notes: MB embedded_mbid (authoritative) confirms title, artist The Blue Notes, label Ogun Recordings, release-group first release 1976, and all 4 keyTracks exactly. The embedded release is dated 2022-06-10, consistent with the prose's claim of a 2022 Ogun reissue. Unverifiable from pack: personnel (Pukwana/McGregor/Dyani/Moholo), recording date and place (December 23 1975, London rehearsal room), double-album format, and the 2008 'The Ogun Collection' compilation claim. No contradictions found.

Stored Wikipedia page entity-matches ('1976 studio album by Pat Metheny') and verifies verbatim: debut album, recorded December 1975, released on ECM in March of the following year, trio with Jaco Pastorius and Bob Moses (personnel section confirms). MB search (similarity 1.0) confirms 1976, ECM Records, and all 4 keyTracks on the tracklist. Remaining prose ('launched... influential career', 'influenced a generation') is editorial.

MB search (similarity 1.0) confirms title, artist David Murray, year 1976, label India Navigation (IN 1026), and all 4 keyTracks exactly; release group secondaryType is Live, which the prose does not contradict. No Wikipedia evidence. Unverifiable: debut-album status, Albert Ayler tribute, tenor saxophone, New York loft-scene framing. No contradictions.

fieldIssues.ours: Prolegomenon (intro); Vox Occultus; Kha'laam; Impaled; Corrumpert Interludium; Vile Temptress
fieldIssues.evidence: These six titles are, in order, the first six tracks of a 2015 release by a DIFFERENT artist also named Gateway (no label, first released 2015-07-23), not the 1976 ECM jazz trio album
fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: releaseGroup firstReleaseDate: "2015-07-23"; tracks: "Prolegomenon (intro)", "Vox Occultus", "Kha'laam", "Impaled", "Corrumpert Interludium", "Vile Temptress", "Hollow", "The Shores of Daruk"; labels: "[no label]"

notes: The albumDNA prose verbatim-matches the entity-correct stored Wikipedia page ('1976 studio album by Gateway', jazz trio John Abercrombie/Dave Holland/Jack DeJohnette, recorded March 1975, released on ECM the following year) -- prose, year, and label are verified. But the MB search hit is a different entity: a 2015 self-released album by another band named Gateway, and our keyTracks are exactly that album's first six tracks in order. Tracks first released 2015-07-23 cannot be key tracks of a 1976 album, so the keyTracks field was populated from the wrong entity. The real 1976 album's tracklist is not in the pack (the wiki extract's track-listing section is empty), so correct keyTracks must be re-sourced.

Stored Wikipedia page entity-matches ('1976 studio album by Kenny Wheeler') and verifies verbatim: Canadian trumpeter and composer, recorded June 1975, released on ECM the following year, quartet with Keith Jarrett, Dave Holland and Jack DeJohnette. MB search (similarity 1.0) confirms 1976, ECM Records (ECM 1069), and all 3 keyTracks ('Smatter' matches MB "'Smatter" punctuation-insensitively). Remaining prose is editorial.

MB search hit is a different release group -- a 1997 Warner Jazz France compilation also titled 'Jaco Pastorius' (secondaryTypes: Compilation, firstReleaseDate 1997) -- discarded for fact-checking. Stored Wikipedia page entity-matches ('1976 studio album by Jaco Pastorius') and verifies: debut solo album, released 1976 by Epic Records, produced by Bobby Colomby; all 4 keyTracks (Donna Lee, Come On Come Over, Continuum, Portrait of Tracy) appear in the wiki per-track personnel listing. Ours calls Colomby 'founder' of Blood, Sweat & Tears where wiki says 'original member' -- compatible wording, not a contradiction. Verification rests on the wiki page only.

MB search (similarity 1.0) confirms year 1976, label Milestone (M-9065), artist Flora Purim, and all 6 keyTracks ('Times Lie' = MB "Time's Lie" punctuation-insensitively). No Wikipedia evidence. Unverifiable: Airto Moreira on percussion, 'four-octave range', Brazilian-vocalist biography, West Coast personnel claim. No contradictions.

'My Foolish Heart' is absent from the wiki's complete LP tracklist -- possible contamination from another Fukui album (suspicion only, not citable); a reissue bonus track cannot be ruled out from pack evidence. Unverifiable: label Trio Records, recorded at age 28, Sapporo origin, 2010s YouTube-virality claim.

Prose matches the stored wiki page verbatim, including the Mosaic Select 2007 CD claim and the '6 original pieces' sentence. Wiki short description: '1976 studio album by Bobby Hutcherson', Blue Note label confirmed. MB embedded_mbid (authoritative) is a 2013 Japanese Blue Note reissue of the same release group (firstReleaseDate 1976); all 5 keyTracks appear on both the MB and wiki tracklists.

fieldIssues.evidenceQuote: recorded in 1976 and first released on the Muse label

notes: Stored wiki ('1976 studio album by Pat Martino') entity-matches and verifies: recorded 1976, first released on Muse, Martino on guitar. MB embedded_mbid (authoritative) confirms first release 1976-02-13 and all 5 keyTracks; MB carries no label entry. The site's label FIELD ships the placeholder 'Unknown' even though our own prose and wiki both say Muse -- repair: set label to Muse (placeholder treated as missing value, not a wrong claim). Verdict driver: the sentence 'jazz-rock fusion era, embracing electric instruments and rhythms from rock and funk' is a substantive characterization that cannot be checked and sits oddly against the evidence (wiki reviews describe a subdued, tender ballads session by a guitar + electric-piano duo: 'tender side in a more subdued setting'); no verbatim contradiction exists, so it is unverifiable rather than an error.

Prose verbatim-matches the stored wiki page ('1977 studio album by Azimuth', British trio John Taylor/Norma Winstone/Kenny Wheeler, recorded March 1977, ECM) -- entity, year, label, personnel verified. MB search instead matched a 1975 Polydor EP by the Brazilian band Azimuth/Azimuth (different entity; tracks 'Zombie', 'Melo da cuica', etc.) -- discarded. RED FLAG for repair: all six of our keyTracks are Brazilian-Portuguese titles ('Linha do horizonte', 'Brazil', 'Estrada dos deuses'...) that fit the Brazilian band Azymuth's repertoire, not a British ECM chamber-jazz trio, and the wiki extract names 'The Tunnel' and the title track 'Azimuth' as album tracks -- neither is in our keyTracks. No entity-matched tracklist exists in the pack, so per the rules this cannot be flagged as a proven mismatch, but the keyTracks almost certainly came from the wrong entity and need re-sourcing.

No Wikipedia evidence. Unverifiable: the Nana Vasconcelos duet claim and instrumentation details. No contradictions.
